vulnerability.title: Disable Accepting ICMP Redirects for All IPv6 Interfaces
vulnerability.description: To set the runtime status of the net.ipv6.conf.all.accept_redirects kernel parameter, run the following command: $ sudo sysctl -w net.ipv6.conf.all.accept_redirects=0 To make sure that the setting is persistent, add the following line to a file in the directory /etc/sysctl.d: net.ipv6.conf.all.accept_redirects = 0
vulnerability.recommendation: Remediation script: # Remediation is applicable only in certain platforms if rpm --quiet -q kernel-core; then # Comment out any occurrences of net.ipv6.conf.all.accept_redirects from /etc/sysctl.d/*.conf files for f in /etc/sysctl.d/*.conf /run/sysctl.d/*.conf /usr/local/lib/sysctl.d/*.conf; do # skip systemd-sysctl symlink (/etc/sysctl.d/99-sysctl.conf -> /etc/sysctl.conf) if [[ "$(readlink -f "$f")" == "/etc/sysctl.conf" ]]; then continue; fi matching_list=$(grep -P '^(?!#).*[\s]*net.ipv6.conf.all.accept_redirects.*$' $f | uniq ) if ! test -z "$matching_list"; then while IFS= read -r entry; do escaped_entry=$(sed -e 's|/|\\/|g' <<< "$entry") # comment out "net.ipv6.conf.all.accept_redirects" matches to preserve user data sed -i --follow-symlinks "s/^${escaped_entry}$/# &/g" $f done <<< "$matching_list" fi d

triage_decision.reason: Disabling ICMP redirects for IPv6 is a standard sysctl hardening measure that does not affect authentication, authorization, or critical services. It only changes a kernel networking parameter and can be applied safely without risk of lockout.
triage_decision.estimated_impact: networking affected; Potentially reduces ability to receive legitimate ICMP redirects, but this is a security benefit rather than a risk.

# sysctl settings are defined through files in
# /usr/lib/sysctl.d/, /run/sysctl.d/, and /etc/sysctl.d/.
#
# Vendors settings live in /usr/lib/sysctl.d/.
# To override a whole file, create a new file with the same in
# /etc/sysctl.d/ and put new settings there. To override
# only specific settings, add a file with a lexically later
# name in /etc/sysctl.d/ and put new settings there.
#
# For more information, see sysctl.conf(5) and sysctl.d(5).

previous_review_verdicts.feedback: The plan will correctly disable the ICMP redirects and maintain persistence, but it could be refined to avoid adding redundant files if the setting already exists.
concerns: Potential duplicate configuration lines in /etc/sysctl.d/ if the key is already defined
suggested_improvements: Check for an existing definition in /etc/sysctl.d/ before creating a new file; set correct permissions (0644) on the new config file; verify the runtime value after applying sysctl -w; consider using a deterministic file name that follows an established naming convention.
